channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: The Long Journey From the Strait of Hormuz to the Gas Tank
item.description: Even if the Strait of Hormuz reopened today, oil would still take more than a month to reach consumers. The economic shock from the Iran war could take far longer to ease.
item.dc:creator: Agnes Chang and Pablo Robles

item.title: Los altos precios del Mundial no asustan a los argentinos
item.description: Los precios dinámicos de las entradas han convertido el torneo de este año en el más caro de la historia, lo que ha llevado a los hinchas argentinos a situaciones extremas.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja, Emma Bubola, Lucía Cholakian Herrera and Sarah Pabst

media:content.media:description: Hinchas de River Plate en el Estadio Monumental de Buenos Aires, en el partido de clubes más importante de Argentina contra su rival, Boca Juniors, el mes pasado.

item.title: ¿El autobronceador hace daño?
item.description: Los productos de bronceado sin sol se anuncian como más seguros que el bronceado tradicional al sol. Consultamos a expertos para saber si representan algún riesgo para la salud.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Wenner Moyer

media:content.media:credit: Eric Helgas para The New York Times

item.title: Cómo tu intestino puede afectar a tu cerebro
item.description: Hemos preguntado a científicos y médicos qué hay que saber sobre la conexión entre la salud del intestino y la niebla mental.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

item.title: Price Tags on Teeth
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Mamdani Wants Free Buses for All. The City Council Has Different Ideas.
item.description: Rather than making city buses free for all riders, elected officials and transit advocates are pushing to expand a transit program for low-income New Yorkers.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:credit: Thalia Juarez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Fair Fares program charges low-income New Yorkers half price to take mass transit, but many of those who are eligible are not enrolled.

item.title: Will New York Freeze the Rent? A Panel Will Cast Its First Vote.
item.description: The panel that regulates rents for nearly one million apartments is set to weigh in on potential increases for the first time since Mayor Zohran Mamdani took office.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:credit: Anna Watts for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: New York City tenants and activists protested at a public meeting last year, during which the Rent Guidelines Board approved increases for rent-stabilized tenants.

item.title: N.Y.C. Schools Could Lose 153,000 Students in Next Decade, Study Finds
item.description: A confluence of factors is causing public schools in New York and across the country to experience dramatic reductions in enrollment.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag
item.category: New York City School Construction Authority

media:content.media:description: New York City’s public schools have lost more than 123,000 students since the Covid-19 pandemic.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 7, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Late Night Isn’t Buying Trump’s New ‘Skirmish’ in Iran
item.description: After Trump used the terms “miniwar” and “excursion” to describe the conflict in Iran, Jimmy Kimmel predicted that the president would next call the war “a tiff. It will be a $200 billion tiff.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Oil Prices Hold Steady as Iran Talks Remain Murky
item.description: Investors waited for news on the situation in the Middle East, as the U.S. and Iran gave mixed signals on peace negotiations.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Qué significa entrepreneur
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 536 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: entrepreneur
item.description: This word has appeared in 536 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: 5 Takeaways From the Last Televised California Governor Debate
item.description: Candidates debated housing and insurance policy in the first half, then furiously attacked one another at the end.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla and Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:description: Seven candidates competing to be the next governor of California met for a debate at the Skirball Cultural Center in Los Angeles on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Keeps Saying He Wants to Leave NATO. Maybe He Already Has.
item.description: For the NATO deterrent to work, Europe and Russia both have to believe that America is coming.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Major

item.title: ¿Qué dice la supuesta nota de suicidio de Epstein?
item.description: La nota estuvo sellada durante años como parte del caso penal del compañero de celda de Jeffrey Epstein. The New York Times no ha autenticado que Epstein la haya escrito.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser, Jan Ransom and Steve Eder

item.title: Starmer Braces for Local Election Losses Amid New Era of UK Politics
item.description: Polls predict historic losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party on Thursday as anti-immigrant Reform U.K. makes gains, and a new era of multiparty politics takes shape.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear and Stephen Castle

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A mural showing Aneurin Bevan, the Labour Party hero who helped create the U.K.’s National Health Service, in Tredegar, Wales.

item.title: Starmer Faces a Major Test in a Set of UK Elections: What to Know
item.description: Local elections in England, and parliamentary elections in Scotland and Wales, take place on Thursday. The governing Labour Party is expected to do very badly.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Rousseau/Press Association, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain at the launch of the Labour Party’s local election campaign, in Wolverhampton, England, in March.

item.title: Corrections: May 7, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, May 7, 2026.

item.title: War and Energy Shortages Boost China’s Influence in Asia
item.description: The war in Iran has left China’s neighbors appealing for help, handing Beijing the kind of sway it has long sought.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Oil storage tanks at a depot in Hong Kong. Shipments of Chinese oil products to select countries have offered relief during the war and underscored how Beijing engenders good will in times of crisis, experts say.

item.title: The ‘Perfect Storm’ Hanging Over Britain’s Public Debt
item.description: Bond investors are expected to scrutinize the results of critical local elections on Thursday as the war in the Middle East adds inflation pressure on the government.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson

media:content.media:credit: Joseph Horton for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Investors are betting the Bank of England will approve several interest rate increases this year, reversing previous expectations that rates would come down.

item.title: Quote of the Day: As World’s Powers Focus on Arctic, Soldiers Get Ready to Fight in Cold
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, May 7, 2026.

item.title: Pacientes de hantavirus aterrizan en Ámsterdam. Se planean más evacuaciones del crucero
item.description: Se ofrecerá a los pasajeros de otros países de la Unión Europea la posibilidad de evacuar a sus propios ciudadanos, según un portavoz del Ministerio de Salud español.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses, Aimee Ortiz and Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:description: Two patients were flown to the Netherlands on Wednesday for medical treatment.

item.title: Federal Agents and Los Angeles Police Arrest 18 in Drug Raid
item.description: Federal and local officers raided businesses on Wednesday. Last summer, immigration tensions flared between federal and local officials at the same park.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:description: Drug Enforcement Administration agents near MacArthur Park on Wednesday after the authorities made arrests on charges related to illicit drug sales.

item.title: Judge Says F.B.I. Can Keep 2020 Election Records Seized From Georgia
item.description: The ruling is the latest in a saga driven by President Trump’s desire for redemption in the state, which he lost in 2020. The county is likely to appeal.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Richard Fausset

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The F.B.I. searched an election center in Fulton County, Ga., in January.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 07, 2026
item.description: John Guzzetta and Jeff Chen reunite to give us a Thursday workout.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:description: Make some noise for this Thursday challenge from two prolific constructors.

item.title: Susan Collins Says She Has Long Had a Benign Tremor
item.description: The Republican senator from Maine, running for re-election at age 73 in one of this year’s top Senate races, made the disclosure after mounting online scrutiny on the left.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:description: Senator Susan Collins of Maine, a Republican, is likely to face Graham Platner, a 41-year-old Democrat and a political newcomer, in the November general election.

item.title: Polls in California Governor’s Race Reflect a Chaotic Field
item.description: Four candidates have emerged as leaders in the primary election to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler

item.title: Watch Live: California Governor’s Debate
item.description: Candidates for California governor meet on the debate stage with less than a month until the primary.
item.dc:creator: NBC4 and Telemundo 52

item.title: A Scaled-Back Celebration for Public Servants ‘in Trauma’
item.description: The Trump administration has eliminated more than 350,000 federal jobs. But an annual effort to inspire talented people to serve the public continues.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Williamson
item.category: National Museum of the American Indian

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Samuel J. Heyman Service to America Medals are considered the government equivalent of an Oscar and go to civil servants nominated by their bosses and colleagues.

item.title: Watercraft Slams Into Gray Whale Near Vancouver, Injuring Driver
item.description: The whale appeared to be unharmed by the collision, which was captured on video.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

item.title: Anthropic’s C.E.O. Says It Could Grow by 80 Times This Year
item.description: The chief executive, Dario Amodei, said the rapid growth had exponentially increased the start-up’s need for more computing power.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha

media:content.media:description: Anthropic’s chief executive, Dario Amodei, in January. On Wednesday, he spoke at his company’s annual developer conference.

item.title: Health Authorities Monitor Hantavirus Cruise Passengers in United States
item.description: People in at least three states are being monitored after being on the ship. None have shown symptoms, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk and Nina Agrawal
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: Three sick people aboard the MV Hondius were evacuated on Wednesday.

item.title: Candidates for California Governor Trade Barbs and Talk Policy
item.description: Five Democrats and two Republicans met onstage less than a month before an open primary that will advance two of them to the general election.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Gyrations on the War Leave Even Rubio Out of Sync
item.description: The administration’s latest shifts on the status of the conflict show how treacherous it is to speak for a president who cultivates an erratic style.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio holding a briefing at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: The Boxing Match That Killed a Police Recruit
item.description: A Massachusetts State Police cadet, already hobbled from a previous bout, raised his hand to take on a more skilled fighter, and investigators say no one stopped him. Four people face charges.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker and Aimee Ortiz

media:content.media:description: Jennifer Penton of the Massachusetts State Police, left, and Troopers David Montanez, center, and Edwin Rodriguez were arraigned on charges of involuntary manslaughter in the death of Enrique Delgado-Garcia last month.

item.title: ‘The Book of Mormon’ Will Close for 2 Weeks After Fire
item.description: The long-running musical, one of Broadway’s biggest hits, will be closed through at least May 17 after an electrical fire in its lighting booth.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson

media:content.media:credit: New York City Department of Buildings
media:content.media:description: Fire damage at the Eugene O’Neill Theater, which has housed “The Book of Mormon” for 15 years.

item.title: JPMorgan Tried to Settle Harassment Accusations Against Executive
item.description: The bank offered $1 million before a former employee filed a lawsuit against a woman who was an executive director on his team, two people briefed on the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland

media:content.media:description: A New York lawsuit accuses JPMorgan Chase of allowing one of its investment bankers to make sexual advances and racially motivated comments to a lower-ranking employee.

item.title: Si la deforestación sigue, la Amazonía estaría en un punto sin retorno
item.description: Un nuevo estudio destaca la letal combinación de pérdida de árboles y calentamiento global que podría llevar a que el pulmón del mundo colapse a mitad de siglo.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey

media:content.media:description: Un camión maderero rodando por una sección deforestada del Amazonas en el estado de Pará, Brasil, en noviembre.

item.title: Lady Gaga and Doechii Strut, but for Whom?
item.description: From hip house to dark pop, the girls are collaborating. Our critic Jon Caramanica unpacks “Runway,” a track from “The Devil Wears Prada 2” that has Lady Gaga and Doechii joining forces.
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica, Arjun Srivatsa and Joe Coscarelli

item.title: Kendall Myers, estadounidense que espiaba para Cuba, muere a los 88 años
item.description: Mientras trabajaba para el Departamento de Estado, entregó información ultrasecreta a sus contactos cubanos durante 30 años. Fue condenado a cadena perpetua.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

media:content.media:description: Kendall Myers en 2009 antes de su detención ese mismo año. “No pretendíamos hacer daño a ningún estadounidense en particular”, dijo antes de ser condenado. “Nuestro objetivo era ayudar al pueblo cubano a defender su revolución”.

item.title: Elon Musk’s Confidante Shivon Zilis Is Cast as His Inside Source at OpenAI
item.description: Shivon Zilis worked closely with Elon Musk while she was on OpenAI’s board. Her ties to the world’s richest man were detailed in a landmark trial on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Brennan Smart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Shivon Zilis, a longtime confidante of Elon Musk and the mother of four of his children, testified in a federal trial on Wednesday.

item.title: Giuliani Seeks to Have Health Care Covered by 9/11 Program
item.description: The former New York City mayor, who is improving after a serious case of pneumonia, suffers from a respiratory condition linked to his exposure to toxins at ground zero, his spokesman says.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein and Jonah E. Bromwich

media:content.media:description: Rudolph W. Giuliani, the former New York City mayor, attended the ceremony commemorating the 24th anniversary of the Sept. 11, 2001, terrorist attacks.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein’s Purported Suicide Note Is Released by Federal Judge
item.description: A federal judge released the note, which Jeffrey Epstein’s former cellmate said he found in a graphic novel. The New York Times has not authenticated that Mr. Epstein wrote it.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser, Jan Ransom and Steve Eder

item.title: Diplomáticos, espías y guacamayas convergen en este hotel de Caracas
item.description: El JW Marriott se ha convertido en la embajada de facto para diplomáticos estadounidenses mientras inversionistas buscan su siguiente gran negocio en el país sudamericano en el lobby.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero and Todd Heisler

media:content.media:description: El último piso del Marriott es la sede de facto de la embajada estadounidense en Caracas.

item.title: Trump’s Indiana Victory Shows His Enduring Grip on Core Republican Supporters
item.description: Low approval ratings? MAGA divisions? The president was able to turn out party loyalists in an Indiana primary to help him oust Republican state lawmakers who had crossed him.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Elecciones primarias de Indiana: los candidatos de Trump se impusieron
item.description: El presidente apoyó a los adversarios de siete legisladores republicanos que se opusieron a su iniciativa de redistribución de distritos. Al menos cinco ganaron sus elecciones.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

media:content.media:description: Votantes en las urnas en West Lafayette, Indiana, el martes
